name: Gyazo MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from nota (Gyazo's parent company) for Gyazo — a popular screenshot and image capture service with cloud storage, OCR, and sharing capabilities. Enables AI agents to access captured screenshots, search image content via OCR, retrieve image metadata, and interact with a user's Gyazo image library.
use_cases: Accessing and searching screenshot history from productivity and context agents
Retrieving OCR text from captured images for information extraction agents
Querying Gyazo image library for visual context in AI workflows
Integrating screenshot history into AI-assisted knowledge management workflows
not_for: Teams not using Gyazo for screenshot capture
best_when: A developer or knowledge worker using Gyazo wants AI agents to access their screenshot history — searching captured images by OCR content, retrieving context from past captures, or integrating visual history into AI workflows.
avoid_when: You use other screenshot tools (Snagit, ShareX, native macOS/Windows capture). Gyazo-specific tool from official nota org.

auth.notes: Gyazo OAuth 2.0 or API token required. Register application at Gyazo developer portal.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Gyazo free: unlimited captures with basic features; OCR and advanced features on paid plans
pricing.paid_tiers: Gyazo Pro/Team for OCR, longer capture history, team features
pricing.notes: Gyazo has a generous free tier. OCR search requires Gyazo Pro. MCP server is free open source from official nota org.

performance.rate_limits: Gyazo API rate limits by plan

known_agent_gotchas: OCR search requires Gyazo Pro subscription — verify plan before relying on text search
Screenshot images are stored in JP/US — consider data residency for sensitive captured content
Official nota org provides quality implementation — expect stable API
Gyazo data includes potentially sensitive screenshots — restrict agent access appropriately
agent_readiness.security_notes: Official nota/Gyazo integration. OAuth auth. HTTPS. Screenshot images may contain sensitive information (passwords, private data visible in captures). Restrict access scope.

security_score_components.security_notes: Official Gyazo. OAuth. HTTPS. Screenshot privacy — images may contain sensitive on-screen data.
